name: toolkit-leopoldo
description: Curated engineering toolkit: opinionated stack of plugins for disciplined coding, shared domain language, backlog flow, and communication modes. Adapted in part from mattpocock/skills (MIT).

plugins.name: grill-me
plugins.description: Stress-test plans through relentless interviewing — walks the decision tree branch by branch, one question at a time, exploring the codebase when answers live there. Triggers: 'grill me', 'pressure test', 'poke holes', 'play devil's advocate', 'validar plan', 'discutir antes de implementar'.

plugins.name: engineering-discipline
plugins.description: Disciplined coding stack: /diagnose (6-phase debugging loop), /deep-modules (architecture review using depth/seam/leverage vocabulary), /tdd-vertical (red-green-refactor with tracer bullets), /zoom-out (higher-level codebase mapping). Shared vocabulary across all four.

plugins.name: shared-language
plugins.description: Shared domain language tooling: /grill-with-docs (grilling that updates CONTEXT.md inline + offers ADRs sparingly) and /context-bootstrap (bootstrap a CONTEXT.md from an existing codebase). Pairs with adr-sync.

plugins.name: backlog-flow
plugins.description: Backlog pipeline from PRD to AFK agent: /to-prd (synthesize a PRD from current context), /to-issues (vertical-slice tracer-bullet breakdown), /triage (state-machine for issue roles), /agent-brief (durable handoff format).

plugins.name: response-modes
plugins.description: Optional output styles for response tone/format. Caveman: ultra-terse, drops filler. No-TLDR: overrides the always-on TL;DR rule for fluid chat. Toggle via /output-style or settings.
